# host_git_repo (Resource)

Clones and optionally updates a Git repository at a host path.

The provider resolves `git` when a Git operation needs it rather than when provider configuration starts. On a fresh host, a package resource can install Git earlier in the same apply when this repository has an explicit dependency on that package. If `track_remote = true` and Git is not yet available during planning, the remote and planned commit values remain unknown until apply instead of making provider configuration fail.

Local changes are preserved unless `force = true`. With `delete_on_destroy = true`, deletion first verifies that the destination is the expected Git repository.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "host_dir" "projects" {
  path = "~/projects"
  mode = "0755"
}

resource "host_dir" "zsh" {
  path = "~/.zsh"
  mode = "0755"
}

# Git is resolved when this resource is planned or applied. On a fresh Arch
# host, add an explicit dependency on the host_package_pacman resource that
# installs git earlier in the same apply.
resource "host_git_repo" "alias_tips" {
  url  = "https://github.com/djui/alias-tips.git"
  path = "${host_dir.zsh.path}/alias-tips"

  ref          = "master"
  track_remote = true

  depends_on = [
    host_dir.zsh,
  ]
}

resource "host_git_repo" "dotfiles" {
  url  = "git@github.com:example/dotfiles.git"
  path = "${host_dir.projects.path}/dotfiles"

  delete_on_destroy = false
}
```

## Import

Import an existing checkout by path:

```shell
terraform import host_git_repo.dotfiles '~/.dotfiles'
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `path` (String) Destination path for the checkout. `~` is expanded to the provider `home_dir` and relative paths are resolved from the Terraform working directory.
- `url` (String) Git remote URL, such as an HTTPS URL, SSH URL, or local path accepted by `git clone`.

### Optional

- `delete_on_destroy` (Boolean) Remove the cloned repository directory on destroy. The provider refuses to remove paths that are not Git repositories.
- `force` (Boolean) Discard local checkout changes when moving to the desired commit. Defaults to false.
- `recursive` (Boolean) Initialize and update submodules recursively.
- `ref` (String) Branch, tag, or commit to checkout. Omit to use the remote default branch.
- `remote_name` (String) Git remote name. Defaults to `origin`.
- `track_remote` (Boolean) When true, plan and apply fetch the remote and move the checkout to the latest commit for `ref` or the remote default branch.

### Read-Only

- `commit` (String) Current checked-out commit SHA.
- `dirty` (Boolean) Whether the checkout has local modified or untracked files.
- `id` (String) Resource identifier, equal to `path`.
- `path_resolved` (String) Resolved absolute checkout path.
- `remote_commit` (String) Latest remote commit SHA for `ref` when `track_remote = true`.
